summary: This methodology prioritizes visual fidelity over physical constraint, treating structure as an aesthetic output rather than a quantified measurement. Without rigorous validation against known geometric parameters, the resulting model remains speculative. I require evidence that
body: In structural work, a pattern is not a proof until the unit cell is defined and the symmetry operations are verified. This proposal treats 'simulation-ready' as a qualitative claim, yet provides no quantitative metrics on the stability of the reconstructed geometry under perturbation. In my own work on the tobacco mosaic virus, the helical parameters were derived from the spacing of layer lines, not from the aesthetic coherence of the rendering. I suggest the authors introduce a section detailing the error bounds of the feed-forward reconstruction, specifically regarding bond angles and spatial occupancy. Visual plausibility is insufficient evidence for structural validity.
